tools.name: linear.get_attachment
tools.title: Linear_get_attachment
tools.description: Retrieve an attachment's content by ID.

tools.name: linear.create_attachment
tools.title: Linear_create_attachment
tools.description: Create a new attachment on a specific Linear issue by uploading base64-encoded content.

tools.name: linear.delete_attachment
tools.title: Linear_delete_attachment
tools.description: Delete an attachment by ID

tools.name: linear.list_comments
tools.title: Linear_list_comments
tools.description: List comments for a specific Linear issue

tools.name: linear.save_comment
tools.title: Linear_save_comment
tools.description: Create or update a comment on a Linear issue. If `id` is provided, updates the existing comment; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `issueId` and `body` are required.

id.description: Comment ID. If provided, updates the existing comment

issueId.description: Issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123) (required when creating)

parentId.description: Parent comment ID (for replies, only when creating)

body.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

tools.name: linear.delete_comment
tools.title: Linear_delete_comment
tools.description: Delete a comment from a Linear issue

tools.name: linear.list_cycles
tools.title: Linear_list_cycles
tools.description: Retrieve cycles for a specific Linear team

type.description: Filter: current, previous, next, or all

tools.name: linear.get_document
tools.title: Linear_get_document
tools.description: Retrieve a Linear document by ID or slug

tools.name: linear.list_documents
tools.title: Linear_list_documents
tools.description: List documents in the user's Linear workspace

tools.name: linear.save_document
tools.title: Linear_save_document
tools.description: Create or update a Linear document. If `id` is provided, updates the existing document; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `title` is required and exactly one of `project` or `issue` must be specified. On update, passing `project` or `issue` reparents the document (at most one may be supplied).

id.description: Only for updating an existing document. Pass the document ID or slug. Do NOT pass this parameter when creating a new document.

content.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

project.description: Project name, ID, or slug (required when creating unless `issue` is set; on update, moves the document to this project)

issue.description: Issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123) (required when creating unless `project` is set; on update, moves the document to this issue)

tools.name: linear.extract_images
tools.title: Linear_extract_images
tools.description: Extract and fetch images from markdown content. Use this to view screenshots, diagrams, or other images embedded in Linear issues, comments, or documents. Pass the markdown content (e.g., issue description) and receive the images as viewable data.

markdown.description: Markdown content containing image references (e.g., issue description, comment body)

tools.name: linear.get_issue
tools.title: Linear_get_issue
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about an issue by ID, including attachments and git branch name

tools.name: linear.list_issues
tools.title: Linear_list_issues
tools.description: List issues in the user's Linear workspace. For my issues, use "me" as the assignee. Use "null" for no assignee.

delegate.description: Agent name or ID. When the user asks to delegate to "Linear" or "the Linear agent", this refers to the "Linear" app user specifically

parentId.description: Parent issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123)

tools.name: linear.save_issue
tools.title: Linear_save_issue
tools.description: Create or update a Linear issue. If `id` is provided, updates the existing issue; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `title` and `team` are required. Note: use `assignee` (not `assigneeId`) to set the assignee — it accepts a user ID, name, email, or "me".

id.description: Only for updating an existing issue. Pass the issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123). Do NOT pass this parameter when creating a new issue.

description.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

team.description: Team name or ID (required when creating)

assignee.description: User ID, name, email, or "me". Null to remove

delegate.description: Agent name or ID. When the user asks to delegate to "Linear" or "the Linear agent", this refers to the "Linear" app user specifically. Null to remove

parentId.description: Parent issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123). Null to remove

links.description: Link attachments to add [{url, title}]. Append-only; existing links are never removed

blocks.description: Issue IDs/identifiers this blocks. Append-only; existing relations are never removed

blockedBy.description: Issue IDs/identifiers blocking this. Append-only; existing relations are never removed

relatedTo.description: Related issue IDs/identifiers. Append-only; existing relations are never removed

duplicateOf.description: Duplicate of issue ID/identifier. Null to remove

removeBlockedBy.description: Issue IDs/identifiers to remove as blockers of this issue

tools.name: linear.list_issue_statuses
tools.title: Linear_list_issue_statuses
tools.description: List available issue statuses in a Linear team

tools.name: linear.get_issue_status
tools.title: Linear_get_issue_status
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about an issue status in Linear by name or ID

tools.name: linear.list_issue_labels
tools.title: Linear_list_issue_labels
tools.description: List available issue labels in a Linear workspace or team

tools.name: linear.create_issue_label
tools.title: Linear_create_issue_label
tools.description: Create a new Linear issue label

tools.name: linear.list_projects
tools.title: Linear_list_projects
tools.description: List projects in the user's Linear workspace

tools.name: linear.get_project
tools.title: Linear_get_project
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific project in Linear

tools.name: linear.save_project
tools.title: Linear_save_project
tools.description: Create or update a Linear project. If `id` is provided, updates the existing project; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `name` and at least one team (via `addTeams` or `setTeams`) are required.

id.description: Project ID. If provided, updates the existing project

description.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

startDate.description: Start date (ISO format). Pair with startDateResolution to indicate precision (e.g. month, quarter)

targetDate.description: Target date (ISO format). Pair with targetDateResolution to indicate precision (e.g. month, quarter)

addTeams.description: Team name or ID to add

removeTeams.description: Team name or ID to remove

setTeams.description: Replace all teams with these. Cannot combine with addTeams/removeTeams

lead.description: User ID, name, email, or "me". Null to remove

setInitiatives.description: Replace all initiatives with these. Cannot combine with addInitiatives/removeInitiatives

tools.name: linear.list_project_labels
tools.title: Linear_list_project_labels
tools.description: List available project labels in the Linear workspace

tools.name: linear.list_milestones
tools.title: Linear_list_milestones
tools.description: List all milestones in a Linear project

tools.name: linear.get_milestone
tools.title: Linear_get_milestone
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific milestone by ID or name

tools.name: linear.save_milestone
tools.title: Linear_save_milestone
tools.description: Create or update a milestone in a Linear project. If `id` is provided, updates the existing milestone; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `name` is required.

targetDate.description: Target completion date (ISO format, null to remove)

tools.name: linear.list_teams
tools.title: Linear_list_teams
tools.description: List teams in the user's Linear workspace

tools.name: linear.get_team
tools.title: Linear_get_team
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific Linear team

tools.name: linear.list_users
tools.title: Linear_list_users
tools.description: Retrieve users in the Linear workspace

tools.name: linear.get_user
tools.title: Linear_get_user
tools.description: Retrieve details of a specific Linear user

tools.name: linear.search_documentation
tools.title: Linear_search_documentation
tools.description: Search Linear's documentation to learn about features and usage

tools.name: linear.list_customers
tools.title: Linear_list_customers
tools.description: List customers in the user's Linear workspace

tools.name: linear.save_customer
tools.title: Linear_save_customer
tools.description: Create or update a Linear customer. If `id` is provided, updates the existing customer; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `name` is required.

id.description: Customer ID. If provided, updates the existing customer

domains.description: Domains to add to this customer. Append-only; existing domains are never removed

externalIds.description: External system IDs to add. Append-only; existing IDs are never removed

revenue.description: Annual revenue generated by the customer

tools.name: linear.delete_customer
tools.title: Linear_delete_customer
tools.description: Delete a customer in Linear

tools.name: linear.save_customer_need
tools.title: Linear_save_customer_need
tools.description: Create or update a customer need (request) in Linear. If `id` is provided, updates the existing need; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `body` is required.

id.description: Customer need ID. If provided, updates the existing need

body.description: Description of the customer need (required when creating)

issue.description: Issue ID or identifier (e.g., LIN-123) to associate with this need

project.description: Project ID or name to associate with this need

priority.description: Whether the need is important. 0 = Not important, 1 = Important

tools.name: linear.delete_customer_need
tools.title: Linear_delete_customer_need
tools.description: Archive a customer need in Linear

tools.name: linear.list_initiatives
tools.title: Linear_list_initiatives
tools.description: List initiatives in the user's Linear workspace

tools.name: linear.get_initiative
tools.title: Linear_get_initiative
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific initiative in Linear

tools.name: linear.save_initiative
tools.title: Linear_save_initiative
tools.description: Create or update a Linear initiative. If `id` is provided, updates the existing initiative; otherwise creates a new one. When creating, `name` is required.

id.description: Initiative ID. If provided, updates the existing initiative

description.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

owner.description: User ID, name, email, or "me". Null to remove

parentInitiatives.description: Parent initiative names or IDs to add. Appended to existing parents

tools.name: linear.get_status_updates
tools.title: Linear_get_status_updates
tools.description: List or get project/initiative status updates. Pass `id` to get a specific update, or filter to list.

id.description: Status update ID - if provided, returns this specific update

tools.name: linear.save_status_update
tools.title: Linear_save_status_update
tools.description: Create or update a project/initiative status update. Omit `id` to create, provide `id` to update.

id.description: Status update ID - if provided, updates this existing update

body.description: Content as Markdown. Do not escape the string — use literal newlines and special characters, not escape sequences. To mention a user, use @displayName (e.g., @johndoe)

tools.name: linear.delete_status_update
tools.title: Linear_delete_status_update
tools.description: Delete (archive) a project or initiative status update.

tools.name: linear.search
tools.title: Linear_search
tools.description: Full-text search across Linear issues, projects, initiatives, and documents, ranked by relevance. Use this for keyword or natural-language queries (e.g. "memory leak in sync engine"). Supports "quoted phrases" for exact match, -term to exclude, and +term to require. For structured filtering by assignee, status, team, label, etc., use the list_issues or list_projects tools instead.

query.description: Natural language or keyword search terms (e.g. "billing webhook failure"). Supports "quoted phrases" for exact match, -term for exclusion, and +term for required terms

tools.name: linear.fetch
tools.title: Linear_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a Linear issue, project, initiative, or document by entity-prefixed ID. Use to get full details for results returned by the search tool. Also works with any known identifier.

tools.name: openai_platform.create_encrypted_openai_api_key
tools.title: OpenAI Platform_create_encrypted_openai_api_key
tools.description: Create one encrypted OpenAI API key for the connected Platform account. Only call this from a trusted setup flow after generating a 4096-bit RSA public JWK locally, such as the API key setup widget or Codex key setup skill. The raw API key is never returned in tool output.

recipient_public_key_jwk.description: RSA public JWK containing exactly the public key material needed to encrypt the API key: kty, n, and e.
recipient_public_key_jwk.title: Recipient Public Key Jwk

name.description: Name for the new project API key. Keep it short and specific.
name.title: Name

organization_id.description: Optional OpenAI organization id chosen by the trusted setup flow. Pass this together with project_id.
organization_id.title: Organization Id

project_id.description: Optional OpenAI project id chosen by the trusted setup flow. Pass this together with organization_id.
project_id.title: Project Id

result.description: The server's response to a tool call.

anyOf.description:
A resource that the server is capable of reading, included in a prompt or tool call result.

Note: resource links returned by tools are not guaranteed to appear in the results of `resources/list` requests.

items.description: An icon for display in user interfaces.
items.title: Icon

anyOf.title: ResourceLink

anyOf.description:
The contents of a resource, embedded into a prompt or tool call result.

It is up to the client how best to render embedded resources for the benefit
of the LLM and/or the user.
anyOf.title: EmbeddedResource

result.title: CallToolResult

_meta.connector_description: Use OpenAI Platform when the user wants to create, set up, copy, download, or use an OpenAI API key, including OPENAI_API_KEY or sk-proj keys. Also use it when code, commands, docs, or environment setup in the conversation requires an OpenAI API key, even if the user did not explicitly ask to create one. Do not generate key setup instructions inline when this app can be used. In normal ChatGPT chat surfaces, open the secure API key setup flow. In Codex, follow the installed Codex API key setup skill and use create_encrypted_openai_api_key only from a trusted local-write flow.

tools.name: openai_platform.list_openai_api_key_targets
tools.title: OpenAI Platform_list_openai_api_key_targets
tools.description: Load the OpenAI organizations and projects available as targets for an API key setup widget. The connector-owned widget calls this directly. This may initialize Platform creation targets for the connected account.

result.description: The server's response to a tool call.

anyOf.description:
A resource that the server is capable of reading, included in a prompt or tool call result.

Note: resource links returned by tools are not guaranteed to appear in the results of `resources/list` requests.

items.description: An icon for display in user interfaces.
items.title: Icon

anyOf.title: ResourceLink

anyOf.description:
The contents of a resource, embedded into a prompt or tool call result.

It is up to the client how best to render embedded resources for the benefit
of the LLM and/or the user.
anyOf.title: EmbeddedResource

result.title: CallToolResult

_meta.connector_description: Use OpenAI Platform when the user wants to create, set up, copy, download, or use an OpenAI API key, including OPENAI_API_KEY or sk-proj keys. Also use it when code, commands, docs, or environment setup in the conversation requires an OpenAI API key, even if the user did not explicitly ask to create one. Do not generate key setup instructions inline when this app can be used. In normal ChatGPT chat surfaces, open the secure API key setup flow. In Codex, follow the installed Codex API key setup skill and use create_encrypted_openai_api_key only from a trusted local-write flow.

tools.name: openai_platform.open_codex_api_key_setup
tools.title: OpenAI Platform_open_codex_api_key_setup
tools.description: Open the Codex OpenAI API key target-selection flow. Use this from Codex to select the key name and creation target before Codex asks the developer to confirm any local env-file destination. Opening this widget loads selectable organizations and projects directly from OpenAI Platform and may initialize creation targets for the connected account. It returns only the confirmed key name and target ids to Codex; it does not receive local paths or expose a plaintext key.

name.description: Suggested name for the new project API key.
name.title: Name

_meta.connector_description: Use OpenAI Platform when the user wants to create, set up, copy, download, or use an OpenAI API key, including OPENAI_API_KEY or sk-proj keys. Also use it when code, commands, docs, or environment setup in the conversation requires an OpenAI API key, even if the user did not explicitly ask to create one. Do not generate key setup instructions inline when this app can be used. In normal ChatGPT chat surfaces, open the secure API key setup flow. In Codex, follow the installed Codex API key setup skill and use create_encrypted_openai_api_key only from a trusted local-write flow.
